meta.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
meta.philosophy: Creative and Technology Studies (CTS) is an interdisciplinary field that combines visual arts, performing arts, physical education, health, home economics, technology, and design. It is designed to develop learners' creativity, practical skills, and technological literacy from an early age.
meta.competence_learning: The Creative and Technology Studies Syllabus adopts a Competence-Based Approach. It covers Visual Arts, Performing Arts, Technology, Clothing and Textiles, Home Economics, and Physical Education and Health. The syllabus uses pair work, group/team work, individual work, field trips, project method, discussion method, and guest speakers as teaching approaches.
goals: Develop creativity, practical skills and technological literacy in learners.
Foster collaboration, communication and problem-solving through arts and technology.
Develop physical fitness and healthy living habits.
Promote cultural appreciation and environmental awareness.

specific_outcomes: 2.4.1.1 Manage waste using reduce, reuse and recycle
2.15.1.1 Construct familiar objects using available materials
